content:
# Final Review Aggregate

**Summary:** Final review accepted 4 amendment(s); restart required.
**Restart Required:** true
**Force Completed:** false
**Total Reviewers:** 2
**Unique Amendments:** 4
**Final Review Restarts:** 14/25

## Accepted

- `fr-14-1378ea30`
- `fr-14-0b26b2bc`
- `fr-14-48dbcac3`
- `fr-14-6ceff67a`

## Rejected

None.

## Disputed

None.

## Final Accepted Amendments

### `fr-14-1378ea30`

In `rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal` (service.rs ~line 2433), the function hardcodes `FsMilestoneJournalStore` to read the journal, bypassing the `MilestoneJournalPort` abstraction. The public `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings` accepts a `_mapping_store: &impl PlannedElsewhereMappingPort` but ignores it, and never accepts a journal port parameter. This means the function cannot be tested with an in-memory journal store — tests must always go through the real filesystem. Accept a `&impl MilestoneJournalPort` parameter instead of hardcoding `FsMilestoneJournalStore`.

### `fr-14-0b26b2bc`

In `verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success` (success_reconciliation.rs ~line 722), the filter `m.run_id.as_deref().is_none_or(|rid| rid == run_id)` includes mappings with `run_id: None` (i.e., legacy mappings recorded before the `run_id` field was added, or mappings from the direct `reconcile_planned_elsewhere` path which sets `run_id: None`). These None-run_id mappings will be re-verified on every successful run, potentially posting duplicate `br comment` calls. The Phase 2/Phase 3 persist-then-comment pattern prevents duplicates within a single run, but across separate runs the same None-run_id mapping will be picked up again, verified again, and commented again. Consider either (a) scoping verification to only `run_id == Some(current_run_id)` or (b) skipping comment posting for re-verified mappings that were already `mapped_bead_verified == true` after the initial load (the `filter(!m.mapped_bead_verified)` on line ~743 handles this for the non-reconstructed path, but reconstructed mappings are always unverified).

### `fr-14-48dbcac3`

Persist enough provenance to distinguish planned-elsewhere mappings across final-review rounds/rollback branches within the same run, or defer writing them until the visible final-review result is known. The current change adds only `run_id` to `PlannedElsewhereMapping` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:736-751`, rebuilds state with an identity key that still ignores round/rollback provenance in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2407-2481`, records mappings eagerly on restart rounds in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:3082-3092` / `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7546-7626`, and later reprocesses any mapping whose `run_id` matches in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:719-744`. A single run can span multiple completion rounds and rollback branches (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7379-7392`), so stale planned-elsewhere findings from an earlier round of the same run will still be verified and commented even if the latest final-review aggregate no longer accepts them.

### `fr-14-6ceff67a`

Do not treat the new `MilestoneEventType::Unknown` arm as a rollback/downgrade compatibility fix. The variant is only present in the new checkout at `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:296-301`, but hard rollback still resets the repository to an older SHA in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1793-1810`. That older code will continue deserializing milestone journal lines directly with `serde_json::from_str` in `src/adapters/fs.rs:3064-3082`, so once `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` starts appending `planned_elsewhere_mapped` events in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2378-2389`, a rollback to a pre-feature commit will still make `milestones/*/journal.ndjson` unreadable. This needs a real migration/write-compatibility strategy, not just a catch-all enum variant in the new code.
